mechanism_narrative: Continued contraction in industrial production signals persistent weakness in manufacturing output, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO declined from 108.5 to 106.1 in past 3 months
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises over 1% and SPX still has negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or rank IC becomes positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, it signals severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reached 7.2% in Dec 2008, the highest since 1993
failure_condition: If SPX rises on more than 60% of days when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or if the average 5-day return is positive under that condition.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rates and collapsing retail sales indicates severe demand deficiency and deflation risk, which cannot be fully offset by monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut to 0.16% in Dec 2008; RSAFS down 8% yoy
regime_hint: zero lower bound with demand collapse
failure_condition: If SPX posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and RSAFS declining more than 5% yoy, or rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis zero-lower-bound regime, further monetary easing via fed funds rate cuts is ineffective because rates are already near zero; any remaining easing expectations have already been priced in, so the net effect on equities is negative as the economy continues to deteriorate.
failure_condition: If SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and declining, or if the signal becomes positive when rates rise above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-on-quarter decline in real GDP exceeding 2% signals a deep recession and severe demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2008 GDP released Jan 2009 estimated at -3.8% qoq (preliminary)
failure_condition: If after such GDP declines SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or rank IC over a 3-year rolling window is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent contraction in nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based job destruction that directly reduces household income and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS fell by 681,000 in Dec 2008, accelerating losses
failure_condition: If PAYEMS turns positive (adds jobs) and SPX still falls, or if average 5-day return is positive when PAYEMS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous drop in industrial production and retail sales confirms both supply and demand contraction, amplifying recession severity and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both series declining sequentially for 3 months
failure_condition: If SPX posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline together, or if rank IC over any 2-year window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling to extremely low levels (below 600,000 units annualized) signals a housing market collapse that drags down construction and related industries, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 700,000, or rank IC over a 3-year rolling window is less than 0.03.
